auto_approve.mechanism: Crush does not document pre-configured auto-approval for specific MCP tools

enterprise_management.mechanism: Crush does not document organization-level MCP management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Crush MCP configuration supports environment variable references in server env blocks

marketplace.mechanism: Crush does not have an in-app MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: Crush MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

resource_referencing.mechanism: MCP resource referencing (as distinct from tool calls) not documented in Crush

tool_filtering.mechanism: MCPConfig has a disabled_tools array (list of tool names to exclude from a specific server). This enables per-server tool filtering at the configuration level.

transport_types.mechanism: Crush documents stdio, http, and sse transports in its JSON schema for MCP server configuration; default transport is stdio

providerExtensions.name: Three transport mechanisms
providerExtensions.summary: Supports stdio, http, and sse transports for MCP server connections, configured via the mcp key in crush.json.

providerExtensions.name: Unified crush.json config file
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers, tools, permissions, and model settings all share a single crush.json config file rather than being split across multiple settings files.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server disabled_tools list
providerExtensions.summary: Each MCP server entry in crush.json supports a disabled_tools array to exclude specific tool names from that server's tool registration. This is a per-server block list, not a global filter.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server custom HTTP headers
providerExtensions.summary: HTTP and SSE transport entries support a headers object for attaching custom request headers (e.g. auth tokens) to server connections.
